clinical_information.diagnosis: High-grade osteosarcoma of the distal femur (post surgical resection)
clinical_information.prior_treatments_and_results: Neoadjuvant chemotherapy (Doxorubicin 75 mg/m2 q21d, Cisplatin 120 mg/m2 q21d, Methotrexate 12 g/m2 q14d) given March–April 2024 (3 cycles). Limb-sparing surgical resection of the distal femur with prosthetic implant on May 1, 2024. Adjuvant chemotherapy May–September 2024 (6 cycles) with reduced-dose Doxorubicin 37.5 mg/m2 q21d, Cisplatin 90 mg/m2 q21d, Methotrexate 12 g/m2 q14d, and Ifosfamide 9 g/m2 q21d with Mesna. No imaging response or post-treatment tumor status documented in the note.
clinical_information.symptom_severity_and_impact: Patient reports left thigh pain at the site of prior resection, and 2 weeks of non-productive cough and fatigue. Severity not quantified; symptoms are symptomatic but no documented functional limitations or systemic signs (no fever, no dyspnea).
